Heart O' the Hills

SUMMER CAMP FOR GIRLS 6 THRU 16

Raising Strong and Powerful Girls Since 1953

For over 70 years, Heart O' the Hills has been more than just a summer camp — it’s a community where girls come to grow, challenge themselves, and discover their true potential. Nestled in the heart of the Texas Hill Country, our camp provides a safe, supportive environment that fosters leadership, resilience, and lifelong friendships.

We challenge and empower girls to be confident, compassionate, and courageous

At The Heart, we believe in empowering girls to be confident, compassionate, and courageous. Our diverse programs are designed to inspire adventure, creativity, and personal growth, all while embracing the rich traditions that make our camp a second home to generations of girls.

Our facilities are designed for safety, rooted in tradition, and surrounded by beauty

Our newly constructed cabins, dining hall, and activity spaces were designed with safety front of mind. Campers will experience a summer retreat with spaces that take in exceptional hill country views, far above the floodplain. With office and activity structures dating back to the 1940s, the property offers a rare combination of natural beauty and heritage.

We're located in a beautiful hill country setting along the Guadalupe River

Heart’s new home is along the North Fork of the Guadalupe River, located between Camp Stewart and Camp Waldemar. With 1,800+ feet of riverfront, and 100+ acres, the site offers a unique combination of riverfront access and hilltop beauty. The riverfront lined with Cypress trees embodies the magic that Hunt is known for, while cabins above enjoy long-range views.

Summer 2026

A new chapter begins

Heart O’ the Hills Camp for Girls is opening for Summer 2026 in a new location, under new ownership, and with a renewed commitment to the traditions and spirit that make Heart so special. While the setting will be new, the essence of Heart—the songs, traditions, friendships, and spirit—remains unchanged. Work on infrastructure and cabins is underway with an exceptional construction team, and check out our Updates Page to follow along on construction, see our upcoming events, and find out ways you can contribute.

A new chapter begins

Heart O’ the Hills Camp for Girls is opening for Summer 2026 in a new location, under new ownership, and with a renewed commitment to the traditions and spirit that make Heart so special. While the setting is new, the essence of Heart—the songs, traditions, friendships, and spirit—remains unchanged. Work on infrastructure and cabins is underway with an exceptional construction team. Check out our Updates Page to follow along on construction, see our upcoming events, and find out ways you can contribute.

What families say about us...

“The community at Heart O’ the Hills is truly special. My daughter made lifelong friends and came home with so many wonderful memories. It’s an experience she’ll never forget.”

Mark T. - Parent

“The camp’s commitment to creating a positive, empowering environment is evident in every aspect. My daughter came home with new skills, great stories, and a newfound confidence.”

Michael B. - Parent

“Heart O’ the Hills exceeded all our expectations. The program’s focus on personal development and fun activities made this summer one of the best for my daughter. We’ll definitely be back!”

Brian C. - Parent

“The dedication of the staff at Heart O’ the Hills is amazing. They create an environment where every camper feels valued and supported. My daughter’s growth this summer was incredible.”

Jessica L. - Parent

"Before camp, I was timid & shy. At camp I became open to meeting new people & became exposed to different cultures... today I am an agriculture teacher & FFA advisor instilling those same skills in my own students!"

Stephanie W - Former camper

"I imagine my life without camp and know it would have taken a lifetime to learn the 'soft skills' I learned at Heart O' the Hills. My entire life I've heard “Where ON EARTH did you learn how to do THAT?!” …”Camp!” :)"

Former camper

"We learned how to accept and celebrate one another’s differences... from different languages and religions to simple things like different interests... At some point, you treasure those differences and can't wait to get back to camp!"

Jordan M - Former camper & counselor

"As a young girl I learned how to be brave and do ‘brave things’... It becomes a way of life as so many new and big things happen every day. How many camp-less 6 year olds get that kind of bravery exposure?!?"

Former camper

"I learned an invaluable skill: how to fail. I learned that the work I did on something mattered, and not making it on a first try made it sweeter when success did come. Because of camp, I've always believed that I will get there in time."

Rebecca - Former camper & counselor

"Heart O' the Hills taught me more than I can describe -- for example, how to set a goal and accomplish it, and how to accept loss. These skills stayed with me. Without camp, I would not be where I am today."

Ashlyn T - Former camper

“I can’t thank Heart O’ the Hills enough for the incredible experience my daughter had. The camp’s emphasis on building strong, confident girls is truly inspiring.”

Kevin H. - Parent

“From the exciting activities to the supportive staff, everything about Heart O’ the Hills is designed to inspire and empower. My daughter’s summer was filled with joy and personal growth.”

Laura W. - Parent

“Heart O’ the Hills has set the standard for summer camps. The focus on leadership and personal development is unmatched. My daughter returned home with a new sense of purpose and excitement for the future.”

Rachel S. - Parent

"Thanks to camp, I am independent, a self advocate, and I know how to maintain friendships even from miles away! I learned these things at a young age at camp, and I am forever grateful for it!"

Paige W - Former camper & counselor

"The things I learned from camp are invaluable. The most significant was getting the confidence to speak in front of a crowd... Camp is where my confidence was born and it has stayed with me. I wouldn't be where I am today without camp!"

Ana M - Former camper & counselor

“What a fantastic camp experience! Heart O’ the Hills offers a unique blend of adventure, learning, and personal growth. My daughter loved every minute and came back more confident than ever.”

Natalie J. - Parent

“The camp’s leadership programs are fantastic. My daughter has grown so much in confidence and leadership skills. Thank you, Heart O’ the Hills!”

John D. - Parent

“Heart O’ the Hills was the best summer experience ever! My daughter came home with new friends, new skills, and an amazing attitude. We’ll definitely be coming back next year!”

Sarah L. - Parent

“Heart O’ the Hills is a magical place where girls are encouraged to be their best selves. My daughter has grown so much in her confidence and leadership. We couldn’t be happier!”

Emily R. - Parent

“Heart O’ the Hills provides a perfect blend of adventure, learning, and fun. My daughter’s summer was full of new experiences and lasting friendships. We’re grateful for the impact this camp has had on her.”

Susan M. - Parent

Activities

Our campers stay busy, learning new skills, accepting new challenges, and enjoying all the activities that are available at The Heart -- everything from horseback riding to pottery making, from shooting baskets to paddling canoes. There's something to inspire every camper, no matter what her interests.

LEARN MORE

If you have questions, please call us at 830-353-8673, or fill out the contact form, and we'll get back in touch.


Heart O' the Hills Summer Camp for Girls has previously been accredited by the American Camp Association. We are in the process of attaining accreditation at our new location.
